detector_findings.title: Unchecked low-level call return value
detector_findings.description: Function `deposit` has unchecked low-level call (line 2). The return value of `.call` / `.send` is not verified. If the call fails, execution continues silently. Capture and check: `(bool success,) = addr.call{...}(...); require(success);`

detector_findings.title: Unchecked ERC20 transfer return value
detector_findings.description: Function `deposit` calls `token.transferFrom()` (line 2) without checking the return value. Some ERC20 tokens (USDT, BNB) return `false` instead of reverting on failure. The token transfer may silently fail. Use OpenZeppelin's `SafeERC20` library: `using SafeERC20 for IERC20; token.safeTransfer(...)`

detector_findings.title: Hash collision risk with abi.encodePacked
detector_findings.description: Function `registerPublicKey` uses `abi.encodePacked` with multiple arguments (2 args). encodePacked doesn't pad dynamic types, causing potential hash collisions: `encodePacked("ab","cd") == encodePacked("abc","d")`. Use `abi.encode` instead of `abi.encodePacked`.
detector_findings.snippet:
bytes32 hashedValidator = keccak256(abi.encodePacked(publicKey, msg.sender));
        if (s.validatorPKs[hashedValidator] != bytes32(0)) revert ISSVNetworkCore.IncorrectValidatorStateWithData

detector_findings.title: Hash collision risk with abi.encodePacked
detector_findings.description: Function `validateHashedCluster` uses `abi.encodePacked` with multiple arguments (2 args). encodePacked doesn't pad dynamic types, causing potential hash collisions: `encodePacked("ab","cd") == encodePacked("abc","d")`. Use `abi.encode` instead of `abi.encodePacked`.

detector_findings.title: Hash collision risk with abi.encodePacked
detector_findings.description: Function `hashClusterData` uses `abi.encodePacked` with multiple arguments (5 args). encodePacked doesn't pad dynamic types, causing potential hash collisions: `encodePacked("ab","cd") == encodePacked("abc","d")`. Use `abi.encode` instead of `abi.encodePacked`.

detector_findings.title: Hash collision risk with abi.encodePacked
detector_findings.description: Function `validateClusterOnRegistration` uses `abi.encodePacked` with multiple arguments (2 args). encodePacked doesn't pad dynamic types, causing potential hash collisions: `encodePacked("ab","cd") == encodePacked("abc","d")`. Use `abi.encode` instead of `abi.encodePacked`.

detector_findings.title: Hash collision risk with abi.encodePacked
detector_findings.description: Function `removeValidator` uses `abi.encodePacked` with multiple arguments (2 args). encodePacked doesn't pad dynamic types, causing potential hash collisions: `encodePacked("ab","cd") == encodePacked("abc","d")`. Use `abi.encode` instead of `abi.encodePacked`.

symbolic_findings.title: Potential storage collision in proxy pattern
symbolic_findings.description: Contract uses delegatecall with 5 state variables but no fixed storage slots (ERC1967). State variables in proxy and implementation may collide in the same storage slots.
symbolic_findings.code_snippet: State vars: SSV_STORAGE_POSITION, SSV_STORAGE_PROTOCOL_POSITION, publicKey, sharesData, publicKey

all_findings_unified.title: Potential storage collision in proxy pattern
all_findings_unified.description: Contract uses delegatecall with 5 state variables but no fixed storage slots (ERC1967). State variables in proxy and implementation may collide in the same storage slots.
all_findings_unified.code_snippet: State vars: SSV_STORAGE_POSITION, SSV_STORAGE_PROTOCOL_POSITION, publicKey, sharesData, publicKey

all_findings_unified.title: Hash collision risk with abi.encodePacked
all_findings_unified.description: Function `validateHashedCluster` uses `abi.encodePacked` with multiple arguments (2 args). encodePacked doesn't pad dynamic types, causing potential hash collisions: `encodePacked("ab","cd") == encodePacked("abc","d")`. Use `abi.encode` instead of `abi.encodePacked`.

all_findings_unified.title: Hash collision risk with abi.encodePacked
all_findings_unified.description: Function `hashClusterData` uses `abi.encodePacked` with multiple arguments (5 args). encodePacked doesn't pad dynamic types, causing potential hash collisions: `encodePacked("ab","cd") == encodePacked("abc","d")`. Use `abi.encode` instead of `abi.encodePacked`.
all_findings_unified.snippet:
return keccak256(
            abi.encodePacked(
                cluster.validatorCount,
                cluster.networkFeeIndex,
                cluster.index,
                cluster.balance
